manifest.name: Badge
manifest.description: The Badge component is a versatile UI element designed for concisely displaying supplementary information, status, or numerical counts. It supports various visual `variant` styles (default, secondary, outline, error, warning, success, information) and `fill` options (solid, light) to visually differentiate its purpose and urgency. Key features include the `isCaps` prop for uppercase text with wider letter-spacing, `isNumber` for rendering circular numeric badges (passing the number as children), and `leftIcon`/`rightIcon` for adding visual cues (ignored when `isNumber` is true). The `asChild` prop leverages Radix Slot for flexible composition, allowing the badge to inherit styles while rendering as a custom element like a link or button. It's ideal for marking statuses (e.g., 'Active', 'Pending'), categorizing content (e.g., 'New', 'Beta'), or indicating unread counts (e.g., '8', '99+').

valueDescriptions.default: Standard badge style for general informational purposes.
valueDescriptions.secondary: Subdued badge style, often used for less prominent information.
valueDescriptions.outline: Badge with a border and transparent background, drawing less attention.
valueDescriptions.error: Badge indicating a critical issue or error state (red).
valueDescriptions.warning: Badge indicating a potential issue or warning state (orange/yellow).
valueDescriptions.success: Badge indicating a successful operation or positive status (green).

valueDescriptions.solid: Badge with a solid background color, providing strong visual emphasis.
valueDescriptions.light: Badge with a light background color, often a tinted version of the variant color, offering a softer appearance.

behaviors.name: isCaps
behaviors.description: When true, uses uppercase text with wider letter-spacing.

behaviors.name: isNumber
behaviors.description:
When true, renders as a circular number badge.
Pass the number as children.
@example ```tsx
<Badge isNumber>8</Badge>
<Badge isNumber variant="error">99+</Badge>
```

slots.name: leftIcon
slots.description:
Icon to display before the label.
Icon is automatically sized to 14px (3.5 spacing units).
Ignored when `isNumber` is true.
@example ```tsx
<Badge leftIcon={<IconCheck />}>Verified</Badge>
```

slots.name: rightIcon
slots.description:
Icon to display after the label.
Icon is automatically sized to 14px (3.5 spacing units).
Ignored when `isNumber` is true.
@example ```tsx
<Badge rightIcon={<IconX />}>Dismiss</Badge>
```

other.name: asChild
other.description: When true, renders as child element using Radix Slot. Useful for composition with links or custom elements. @example ```tsx Active ```

guidance.whenToUse: Use the Badge component to highlight important status, categorize items, show counts, or provide quick, at-a-glance information in a compact form. Ideal for notifications, tags, or small labels.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Do not use for complex interactive elements like standalone buttons or links unless carefully composed with the `asChild` prop. For displaying large amounts of text or detailed information, consider using a Tag, StatusLabel, or Tooltip component instead.
guidance.accessibility: Ensure sufficient color contrast for readability across all `variant` and `fill` combinations. When using `isNumber`, ensure the number is concise and easily understandable. If `asChild` is used to compose with an interactive element (e.g., a link or button), ensure the child element provides appropriate ARIA attributes and keyboard accessibility for its role.
